title: Polystichum inaequale
taxonomy: Polystichum inaequale is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Polystichum, which comprises over 260 species of evergreen ferns. The classification of Polystichum inaequale is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant. Polystichum inaequale is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia.

intro: Polystichum inaequale is a species of fern native to China, characterized by its glossy, leathery fronds with multiple pinnae. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Polystichum inaequale include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
